Title: N9 polls: Aminuddin prefers small-scale campaigns to better engage voters
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said he prefers a small-scale campaign approach to enable him to better understand the needs of the local community.
Aminuddin, who is contesting the Linggi seat in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election under Pakatan Harapan (PH), said he has adopted this approach since 2008 to allow him to interact more effectively with voters, while making them feel more comfortable.
"I prefer to move alone or with a small team rather than holding large-scale walkabouts because this provides a more personal space for interaction.
"I feel for the voters. They want to meet the candidates personally, not be surrounded by so many people that they feel uncomfortable," he told reporters after engaging with the Indian community around the Linggi state constituency here today.
During the event, Aminuddin, the Negeri Sembilan PH chairman, crossed paths with Barisan Nasional (BN) incumbent Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli, who was also campaigning in the same area.
Commenting on the response from the Indian community, Aminuddin, who is also the PKR vice-president and state PKR chairman, said the support he has received has been very positive despite Linggi traditionally being a BN stronghold.
The 59-year-old Aminuddin, who is also the Port Dickson Member of Parliament, said although he is currently serving as caretaker menteri besar, the position does not give him any advantage in winning the seat.
"This is not an easy task, and I need to work hard to make inroads into this area by winning the hearts of every voter. I remain optimistic and confident that the people in Linggi will translate this positive support into votes for PH on polling day," he said.
In the state election, Aminuddin is being challenged by Mohd Faizal, 54, and Zamri Md Said, 54, from Bersatu.
The Linggi constituency is located in the southern part of Negri Sembilan and has 20,677 registered voters, comprising 18,420 ordinary voters, with 2,222 military personnel and their spouses, and 35 policemen as early voters.
The state assembly was dissolved on June 5, with the Election Commission setting Aug 1 as polling day and July 28 for early voting.
A total of 103
candidates
from various political parties and independent candidates will contest the 36 seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Yuneswaran calls for greater Indian representation in civil service
Article:
jashadiqe@nst.com.my
Caption: Deputy National Unity Minister R. Yuneswaran called for more Indian youths to pursue careers in the civil service. Courtesy pic: National Unity Ministry.
SEGAMAT: More Indian youths should step forward and pursue careers in the civil service to increase the community's representation in the public sector, Deputy National Unity Minister R. Yuneswaran said.
Yuneswaran, who is also the Segamat MP, said Indians accounted for only 3.7 per cent of the country's civil service workforce in 2024, comprising about 48,190 personnel.
He said greater participation was needed to ensure better representation of the Indian community in government institutions.
"The Indian community's representation in the civil service needs to be increased. More youths should take the opportunity to join the public sector and build their careers there," he said at the Jalinan Mesra Bersama Masyarakat Segamat community engagement programme held at the Jementah multipurpose hall here yesterday.
Yuneswaran said statistics from the Public Service Commission showed that 38,005 Indians applied to join the civil service in 2022, but only 4,516 were called for interviews.
Of those, 3,672 attended the interviews, while only 991 were appointed.
He said some candidates' failure to attend interviews was among the factors affecting Indian participation in the sector.
The programme was organised to guide youths on application procedures, eligibility requirements, examination preparation and interview techniques for civil service recruitment.
The event was attended by graduates as well as Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia and Sijil Tinggi Pelajaran Malaysia  leavers.
It also featured representatives from government agencies, including the National Information Dissemination Centre, Fire and Rescue Department, Health Ministry and the Public Service Commission, who provided information on career opportunities in the public sector.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pocket-size AI: Powerful phones star at China show
Article:
TECH firms are racing to roll out advanced smartphones that use artificial intelligence to do everything from ordering food to composing messages upon a simple voice command.
Wide adoption of phones running on so-called AI agents would be a revolution, but would also take control away from major apps, which aren't always happy about it.
At least three firm were showcasing so-called agentic phones at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai this weekend – heralding what may be to come.
Smartphone maker Nubia unveiled its NaviX Ultra, a phone powered by Doubao, China's massively popular AI chatbot tool run by TikTok creator ByteDance.
"A new era of AI agent smartphones begins," Nubia said, sharing images of the handsets online.
A limited run of a prototype dubbed the "Doubao Phone" sold out fast in December.
Initially, the prototype could follow simple voice commands to execute tasks across apps, including ordering food and comparing shopping prices.
However, days after it was released, tech giants including Alibaba, Tencent and JD.com restricted the built-in assistant's access to their platforms.
The move effectively disabled the phone's AI agent, so ByteDance turned off the powerful tool in certain circumstances, including when payments were involved.
Gaining broad access to apps owned by other companies is a sticking point for AI agent devices, said Kiranjeet Kaur, associate research director at US market intelligence firm IDC.
Platforms want to keep direct contact with their users, otherwise "they lose control to another party", she added.
"Agenting is everyone's dream, but we haven't reached there yet," as the performance of AI agent tools is still often patchy, Kaur said.
According to Chinese tech media, the NaviX Ultra does not attempt to force its way into apps, but rather seeks to collaborate with them.
The first-generation Doubao phone had been hobbled when major apps blocked unauthorised access.
AFP has contacted Nubia for comment.
[[nid:1492486]]
Another manufacturer, Honor, showcased an AI system for its "Robot Phone", whose interactive camera flips up on a small robotic arm.
The company says its "companion-centric" device can interpret human gestures and bop to musical rhythms, as well as take selfies and steady videos.
An agent using several AI models, some co-developed with Alibaba, will be embedded in the robot phone when it goes on sale later this year, Honor told AFP.
Shanghai-based AI startup StepFun also unveiled an "AI agent-native smartphone", the STEPX Neo, ahead of the World Artificial Intelligence Conference.
StepFun's chairman Yin Qi said "deep partnerships" had been established with several major Chinese platforms, including Alipay and ride-hailing giant Didi, according to a sponsored article in state news agency Xinhua.
"Leveraging these services, the smartphone can provide one-stop support for travel bookings, everyday purchases, local services, office productivity and video editing," it said.
Outside China, big tech companies such as Google are busy infusing smartphones with increasingly advanced AI features such as the ability to book appointments.
US startup Brain Technologies launched an agentic "Natural AI Phone" which went on sale in Japan in April in partnership with mobile giant SoftBank Corp.
At a demonstration given to AFP in April, Brain Technologies' phone – which connects with a handful of apps including social network LINE – messaged a contact to apologise for being late on just an audio command, although it also often failed to carry out requests.
"There is no clear winner in this race yet, which is why it is currently quite a hot topic," said Marc Einstein of Counterpoint Research.
But in five or 10 years time, we won't be using apps on our phones "like we do today", he predicted.
"This will fundamentally change the digital economy and disrupt business models."
* The writers are from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PJ's 223 food court traders plead for help after third flash flood this year
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Vendors at the 223 food court here are calling for government intervention after a flash flood yesterday.
This is the third time the food court has been flooded this year.
Jamaliah Yusoff, 62, said yesterday's disaster was the worst yet, with water rising to chin level, surpassing previous incidents on April 20 and May 6.
"Every time it rains, my heart races, seeing our belongings float away is heartbreaking.
"I just bought a new industrial gas stove for RM1,200 two weeks ago, and now it's ruined. We aren't here to make extraordinary profits; we are just trying to support our families," she said.
While local authorities previously provided two months of rent-free assistance to ease their burden, she pointed out that the cost of replacing refrigerators and industrial equipment far exceeds the value of the rent.
The flood struck around 4pm yesterday, catching many by surprise.
Most food vendors had already closed for the day and only learned of the disaster through their WhatsApp group.
During the previous flood on May 6, approximately 60 stalls and several vehicles were submerged in 1.2m-deep water.
Another vendor, 59-year-old Nuraini Zainudin, attributed the rapid flooding to a nearby large drain that overflows almost instantly during heavy downpours.
"It can take only five minutes for the area to be completely flooded.
"Every flood forces us to shut down for two to three days to clean the premises and replace destroyed stock. We are left with nothing but patience and resignation," she said.
Food vendors are now urging the local authorities and the state government to implement a permanent fix for the drainage system in Section 51A to prevent further financial ruin for the local business community.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PJ floods: Residents deserve answers and clear timeline, says MP
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung has yet to receive a formal response from the local authority over the flash floods that hit parts of Petaling Jaya, Selangor, yesterday.
"No formal response yet," Lee told the New Straits Times when asked if he had heard back from the council.
The flash floods, which affected Petaling Jaya and parts of the Klang Valley, mirrored a previous incident on May 6 when several areas, including a Section 51A food court, were inundated.
Following that incident, Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ) said it was planning
long-term upgrades
, including floodwalls and a river overflow control system along Sungai Penchala and Sungai Klang.
However, when asked if there had been visible efforts by the council to address recurring floods since May, Lee said owners of the affected premises had not been briefed on any progress.
"Owners were not briefed," he told the New Straits Times.
In a statement, Lee said that after the flash floods struck in early May, he visited the site to meet affected residents and premise owners.
He said he saw firsthand how underground parking areas were submerged and property damaged.
"To clean up mud, repair damage and restore homes and business premises, many were forced to bear heavy costs, on top of spending a lot of time and energy," he said.
However, he said that just two months later, the same area faced an almost identical situation.
"After the water recedes, what remains is not just mud and damage, but also the frustration and exhaustion of residents who are forced to face the same thing over and over again.
"Every time flooding occurs, they have to clean up again, repair again and bear losses again," he said.
"The question is, until when must this situation continue?" he asked.
He said that following his earlier meeting with residents, he had raised the issue with MBPJ and urged that improvement measures be expedited.
"But after two months, why has this problem still not been resolved?" he questioned.
He said that while some may assume mitigation works were being carried out, the relevant parties needed to provide a transparent explanation to the public if a project was underway.
"Residents do not just need visits and sympathy after a disaster. They have the right to a clear report, the current implementation status and a real timeline for action," he said.
He said MBPJ needed to explain the cause of the floods, announce the progress of current works and outline a concrete follow-up plan.
On the next course of action, Lee said he would organise a meeting between affected premise owners and the city council.
The New Straits Times has also reached out to MBPJ for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three teenage brothers, another teen held over motorcycle thefts in Sg Petani
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Police arrested four teenage boys, three of whom are brothers, on suspicion of stealing motorcycles around the Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd (KTMB) parking area near here on July 14.
Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said preliminary investigations revealed that the suspects, aged 14 to 16, were known as the Vetri Gang and stole parked motorcycles in the area by pushing them as if they were broken.
He said 20 cases of stolen motorcycles have been reported in the area.
"The motorcycle registration plates were not changed but they were used by the suspects, while some were broken down for spare parts," he told reporters at the Kuala Muda police headquarters.
All suspects had no prior criminal records and the head of the gang, 15, did not attend school.
They have been ordered detained under remand till tomorrow for investigations under Section 379A of the Penal Code for theft of a motor vehicle, he added.
Anyone found guilty under the section is liable to a jail term of between one and seven years and a fine.
On another matter, Hanyan said four men, aged 25 to 31, were arrested in Sidam Kiri and Padang Serai on July 14 for suspected involvement in housebreaking and stealing motorcycles.
From them, he said, police seized a kerambit, a fake pistol, nine ball bearings, 0.56g of methamphetamine as well as two Yamaha RXZ motorcycles and various components.
The suspects, who have prior criminal and drug records, are being investigated under sections 457 (housebreaking) and 379A of the Penal Code, he added.
Meanwhile, Hanyan said a series of snatch thefts have been solved with the arrest of a man, 24, in Jalan Tupah, Merbok, recently.
The suspect, who has prior criminal and drug records, was suspected of being involved in 10 snatch thefts in the district, he said, adding that the case is being investigated under Section 394 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt during a robbery.
Police are tracking down another two male suspects believed to be accomplices, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Queen presents aid to police families, asnaf community
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, today officiated the Wilayah Prihatin Uniti Merdeka Contribution presentation ceremony at Dataran Merdeka.
According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's official Facebook page, Her Majesty was received on arrival by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's wife Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, the Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Marhamah Rosli, and Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus.
"At the event, Her Majesty witnessed the handover of donation cheques from the Federal Territory Islamic Religious Council (MAIWP), the Zakat Collection Centre and Habib Jewels Sdn Bhd, with a total value of RM1.7 million to the Kuala Lumpur branch of the police," according to the post.
Raja Zarith Sofiah presented contributions to 30 selected children of police officers and the asnaf community in the Federal Territory.
In addition, the recipients of the donations also received assistance in the form of spectacles, laptops, university entry kits, blood glucose monitors, wheelchairs and blood pressure monitors.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cat rescued from ruins of Venezuela quake offers 'ray of hope'
Article:
CARABALLEDA: A weakened cat was pulled from under the rubble of a collapsed residential complex in Venezuela on Saturday in what a rescuer described as a "ray of hope" in the aftermath of deadly earthquakes.
More than 5,100 people were killed when back-to-back quakes struck the South American nation on June 24, with the coastal state of La Guaira hit hardest.
Volunteer rescue worker Andres Carvajal said he spotted the cat during a search in a La Guaira housing complex that had been reduced to rubble.
"We saw the cat, it got scared and went back inside, deeper into the building," Carvajal told AFP. It was unclear when it had become trapped.
"I went in, took off my glove, put some cat food on my arm... It gradually came closer and, of course, ate with a bit of desperation," the 21-year-old university student said.
Carvajal had written "el gato" – "the cat" – on his helmet, which he said had been his nickname since elementary school.
He and other students from the Central University of Venezuela, in Caracas, formed an association to help with rescue operations.
Once rescued, the cat was handed over to a small camp where vets were on standby. It was given fluids and cleaned before being sent to a shelter.
"I'm very happy we found it," Carvajal said.
"It's impossible not to feel empathy for any life that's here. And finding this little cat is obviously a ray of light, a ray of hope."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke advises Negri election machinery to prioritise road safety
Article:
SEREMBAN: Road safety and traffic compliance must be strictly observed throughout the ongoing 16th Negri Sembilan election campaign, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said.
He stressed that these rules were non-negotiable for preventing accidents and ensuring an orderly, secure environment.
The DAP secretary-general said he had personally instructed his party's campaign teams to wear helmets at all times when riding motorcycles during the campaign period.
"We want to ensure this campaign stays accident-free. Every party wants to win, but not at the cost of flouting traffic laws. The bare minimum is wearing a helmet," he said here today.
"During yesterday's nomination day, I joined a convoy from Pekan Titi to Kuala Klawang. I made it clear upfront — no helmet, no convoy. This is a basic requirement that everyone must adhere to," he added.
Loke said this to reporters after a walkabout with Pakatan Harapan candidates Ho Weng Wah (Temiang), Lee Kai Yet (Mambau), Siau Meow Kong (Rahang), Nicole Tan (Bukit Kepayang), S. Mugunthan (Seremban Jaya), and Chew Seh Yong (Lobak) at Pasar Besar Seremban today.
In a separate reminder, Loke, who is also the Seremban member of parliament, warned candidates and supporters against riding on the tailgates of moving four-wheel-drive vehicles, calling the practice reckless.
He further called on all parties and leaders on the campaign trail to lead by example and strictly observe traffic regulations throughout the campaign.
The Negri Sembilan state assembly was dissolved on June 5, with the Election Commission setting Aug 1 as polling day and July 28 for early voting.
A total of 889,490 registered voters are eligible to vote in this state election, comprising 867,151 ordinary voters, 16,884 military personnel and their spouses, and 5,455 police officers as early voters.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Southern China braces for more rain, Chongqing landslide search continues
Article:
BEIJING: China's meteorological authority on Sunday issued a heavy rain alert for parts of southern China, following a warning a day earlier of mountain flood risks in areas like Chongqing and Yunnan and potential emergency evacuations in vulnerable places.
Cutting south China diagonally, the sprawling rain belt stretches from the southwestern province of Yunnan to the Yangtze River Delta in China's eastern coast.
Rail authorities suspended some passenger trains on the Shanghai–Kunming railway on Sunday due to heavy rain, state broadcaster CCTV reported.
In Yunnan, several scenic spots including the Tiger Leaping Gorge were temporarily closed following heavy rain over the weekend.
Parts of southwestern Guangxi region, still recovering from the impact of Typhoon Maysak earlier this month, was bracing for a new round of rain through Tuesday.
Hydrological authorities in Guangxi's Baise said some rivers rose by one to three metres over the past 24 hours, CCTV reported Sunday.
A rain-triggered landslide in a county in southwest China's Chongqing on Friday has killed eight people, while rescuers are still racing to find 34 people that remain missing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pakistani man fined, car forfeited over undocumented migrants
Article:
PASIR MAS: A Pakistani man was fined RM20,000 by the Sessions Court here today for allowing four undocumented migrants to remain in his car last Sunday (July 12).
Muhammad Zaman, 30, pleaded guilty before Sessions Court Judge Zulkifli Abdullah.
The court also ordered him to serve eight months' imprisonment if he fails to pay the fine.
According to the charge, Muhammad allowed four undocumented migrants to remain in a Proton Gen-2 at a parking area in front of Ariana Exclusive on Jalan Parit Dalam, Kota Baru, at 7.10am on July 12.
The four undocumented migrants were identified as Indian national Anil Kumar, 28, and three Pakistani nationals — Jaan Masih, 37, Ahmed Zahaib, 33, and Mohsin Ali, 23.
Muhammad was charged under Section 55E(1) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for allowing undocumented migrants to remain at a place under his control, an offence punishable under Section 55E(2) of the same Act.
In addition to the fine, the court ordered that the Proton Gen-2 used in the commission of the offence be forfeited to the government.
The prosecution was conducted by Siti Khaidijah Khairuddin.
The accused was unrepresented.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Immigration detains 17 foreigners in Pudu raid, uncovers suspected vice operation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department detained 17 illegal immigrants, including an Indonesian couple suspected of facilitating prostitution, during Op Gegar at the Fraser Business Park area in Pudu early yesterday morning.
In a Facebook post, the department said the operation, conducted at two entertainment outlets beginning at 4am, involved inspections of 203 people comprising both Malaysians and foreigners.
It said 17 foreigners, comprising 10 men and seven women from Indonesia, Bangladesh, and Myanmar, were arrested for suspected offences under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and the Immigration Regulations 1963.
"An examination of a mobile phone belonging to one of the detained undocumented migrants uncovered conversations, transaction records, and a catalogue believed to have been used to offer women for sexual services to clients," it said.
It said the operation was launched following public complaints and two weeks of intelligence gathering. It involved 40 immigration officers, assisted by 10 officers from several enforcement agencies.
During the operation, the Customs Department also seized various brands of alcoholic beverages worth RM11,420.31, with unpaid duties estimated at RM26,380.31.
Immigration said all 17 foreigners were detained under Section 51(5)(b) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 and have been placed at the Putrajaya Immigration Detention Depot pending further investigation and action.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Defence Minister Koizumi says Japan needs debate on nuclear weapons
Article:
TOKYO: Japan's Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi said that Japan needs a debate on nuclear weapons, as some European countries are seeking greater nuclear deterrence, Kyodo News reported.
Japan "cannot avoid touching" the issue, Koizumi said in an online programme released Friday, apparently with the government's planned revision by the end of the year of three key national security documents in mind.
Koizumi cited France and Finland as pursuing policies that favour stronger nuclear deterrence.
Finland's parliament in June passed a bill that would allow nuclear weapons to be brought into the country, while French President Emmanuel Macron said in March that his country will increase the number of nuclear warheads.
Although Japan relies on the US nuclear umbrella, it is the only country to have suffered a nuclear attack and maintains its three principles of not producing, possessing, or allowing nuclear weapons on its territory.
Koizumi said Japan's security environment has grown harsher and argued Japan needs to change a situation in which some topics are deemed unacceptable to debate.
In December last year, a source who is involved in devising security policy under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's government said Japan should possess nuclear weapons, sparking backlash from opposition parties and some countries.
Former Defence Minister Itsunori Onodera also said late last year that Japan needs to debate the future of its non-nuclear principles.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Faith vs therapy: Inside the Philippine school for exorcists
Article:
ASIA'S only dedicated exorcism centre – designed to train not only Philippine priests but clergy from across the region – sits just off a busy thoroughfare in Metro Manila.
Inside, neatly made-up rooms for visiting priests line warmly lit halls that lead to a chapel where the 400-year-old ritual takes place.
Holy relics employed in the proceedings line one wall, another features a one-way mirror that allows family members and novice exorcists to observe.
The Michael Center for Spiritual Liberation and Exorcism – five months old this week – was built because "cases were piling up", Father Jose Francisco Syquia told AFP.
Trauma driven by bullying, sexual abuse and the pain of separation as family members leave for work abroad has increasingly opened the door to spiritual attacks, he said.
"We had to find a more permanent and secure and private place to pray over people," Syquia said.
The centre's function as an effective school for exorcists, however, is what makes it unique.
"I don't know of any other center in the world that trains exorcists," Syquia said of the two-storey facility built largely with donations from local families of the previously afflicted.
Priests from countries including Singapore, Malaysia, Cambodia and India were already lining up for training, Syquia added.
A key element of that training is discerning the difference between psychological and spiritual problems, he said.
While the centre operates without strict Vatican oversight, it follows long-standing Church rules governing exorcism, including mandatory vetting by mental health professionals.
"We have clinical psychologists. We have a psychiatrist... There are also neurologists, so they work hand in hand with us," Syquia told AFP on a visit to the centre.
Still, he insists he knows a demonic possession when he sees one.
"You will see a change in behaviour... a different consciousness. Something else takes over a person," Syquia said, noting he had personally exorcised a demon only a week earlier.
"When it comes to the spiritual world, we need something beyond science."
But attempts to draw that distinction have long worried the mental health community.
"If a condition isn't recognised as medical, it delays appropriate treatment," Christopher French, a psychologist and emeritus professor at the University of London, told AFP.
"It's more dangerous to accept the supernatural explanations," he warned, noting that conditions ranging from schizophrenia to Tourette's syndrome were once interpreted as spirit possession.
[[nid:1492416]]
The Philippines has roughly one psychiatrist for every 200,000 people in the archipelago, according to the World Health Organisation. Some provinces have none at all.
Given the choice between an exorcist and a mental health professional, many in the heavily Catholic country will choose the former, Dr. Kathryn Tan, a Manila-based psychiatrist, told AFP.
"It's a lot of stigma when somebody is brought to a psychiatrist," she said.
"If it's an exorcism... you're a victim, but if you're brought to a psychiatrist, there's a lot of shame," she said.
Many of her own patients came only after first visiting indigenous healers, Tan said, viewing psychiatry as a "last resort."
Tan, who said alleged possessions are often accompanied by symptoms of psychosis, insisted Philippine psychiatrists were not seeking to "compete with the Catholic Church", but rather to bridge the gap between culture and science.
"It's about... compassion and empathy and trying our best to understand without judgement what our patients are experiencing," she said.
Both Tan and French agreed that cultural sensitivities need to be respected in treatment.
"You can shape a treatment to fit the belief system without explicitly endorsing it," French said, conceding an exorcism might even have a beneficial placebo effect in some cases.
Tan, who said she keeps an "open mind" about the supernatural, said it was not surprising when people seek spiritual explanations for something they cannot readily define.
"Perhaps this is our way of trying to control the unknown," she said.
Inside his office, Father Syquia told AFP he was confident science and faith could coexist.
"We're... focusing on the spiritual dimension. But of course, the body is always part of that," he said.
A greater national investment in mental health care might even lighten his own load, he conceded.
"If psychology and psychiatry, if proper mental and emotional health (are) fostered in our country, there will be (fewer exorcism) cases," he said.
"Science is a gift from God."
* The writers are from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zizie Izette to learn fate tomorrow in RM2.8 million corruption case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad will learn her fate tomorrow when the Sessions Court delivers its verdict on three charges of abetting in a RM2.8 million corruption case.
Sessions Court Judge Rosli Ahmad is scheduled to deliver the decision at 9am at the conclusion of the defence case.
Zizie Izette, 47, the wife of the late Kinabatangan member of parliament Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin, faces three charges of abetting her husband in obtaining cash bribes amounting to RM2.8 million linked to a RM150 million investment in Public Mutual Berhad unit trusts.
The defence closed its case after Zizie Izette completed her testimony on March 5. Bung Moktar had testified on Sept 2, 2025, after the couple failed to overturn the Court of Appeal's decision ordering them to enter their defence.
On Nov 18, 2024, the Court of Appeal allowed the prosecution's appeal against the Sept 7, 2023 High Court decision acquitting the couple.
The High Court had earlier overturned the Sessions Court's Sept 2, 2022 ruling, which found that the prosecution had established a prima facie case and ordered the couple to enter their defence.
However, on Jan 16, the Sessions Court acquitted Bung Moktar of all three charges after the prosecution decided not to proceed following the former politician's death on Dec 5, 2025.
Under the first charge, Zizie Izette is accused of abetting Bung Moktar, who was then the non-executive chairman of Felcra Bhd, in obtaining a RM2.2 million cash bribe from Public Mutual Bhd investment agent Madhi Abdul Hamid.
The alleged bribe was an inducement for Bung Moktar to secure the approval of the then second finance minister for Felcra Bhd to invest RM150 million in Public Mutual Berhad unit trust products.
Under the second charge, she is accused of abetting Bung Moktar in obtaining RM262,500 from Madhi through a Public Islamic Treasures Growth Fund account registered in her name for the same purpose.
She also faces a third charge of abetting her husband in obtaining RM337,500 from another Public Mutual Bhd investment agent, Norhaili Ahmad Mokhtar, through a Public Ittikal Sequel Fund account registered in her name.
The offences were allegedly committed at the Public Bank Bhd Taman Melawati branch on June 12 and June 19, 2015.
The charges are framed under Section 28(1)(c) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission Act 2009, which provides for a maximum prison sentence of 20 years and a fine of five times the value of the gratification or RM10,000, whichever is higher, upon conviction.
The prosecution is conducted by deputy public prosecutors Law Chin How and Mohammad Fadhly Mohd Zamry, while lawyer Datuk Seri K. Kumaraendran represented Zizie Izette.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 2027 Budget may see higher Sara allocation, says PM
Article:
IPOH: The government is considering increasing the allocation for the Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (Sara) initiative under Budget 2027, subject to the country's economic position, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Anwar said he had discussed the matter with Treasury secretary-general Datuk Johan Mahmood Merican to assess the feasibility of increasing the assistance to ease the people's burden.
"For recipients of Rahmah Cash Aid (STR) and Sara, I wish to inform you that if the economic situation allows, I discussed yesterday afternoon with the Treasury secretary-general whether we can increase Sara assistance.
"If I go elsewhere and they ask for an increase, whether in Cenderawasih or Baling, I may say, 'Wait first.' But for Tambun, we will give priority, InsyaAllah.
"In the upcoming Budget, I will consider how we can increase allocations to assist the people," he said in his speech at the Madani Kita Programme at Dewan Komuniti Taman Perpaduan here today.
The Tambun MP said the government remained committed to ensuring aid reached those who needed it, especially amid ongoing cost-of-living pressures.
He said that although the government had taken steps to reduce fuel prices, including lowering the price of RON95 petrol and diesel, the prices of certain essential goods remained difficult to control.
"Sometimes, even when fuel prices are reduced, the prices of goods can still be difficult to control.
"For example, onions. We import onions from India and do not produce much locally. So when prices in India increase, our onion prices also go up," he said.
Anwar said such challenges were among issues the government continued to address through various initiatives, including expanding the Rahmah Sales Programme (Jualan Rahmah).
He said the government would continue reviewing its assistance programmes while ensuring responsible financial management.
He said the focus remained on reducing the people's burden and improving their quality of life through targeted initiatives.
The Sara initiative is part of the government's efforts to provide assistance for essential goods to eligible recipients, complementing other measures to address the rising cost of living.
Sara allocations
have risen to RM15 billion in 2026, from RM10 billion in 2024.
Under the Sara Untuk Semua initiative, some 22 million people, or 87 per cent of all recipients, generated over RM1.77 billion in local market transactions this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor Under-12 football tournament player dies during match
Article:
jashadiqe@nst.com.my
Picture: .
JOHOR BARU: A 12-year-old footballer died while playing in an Under-12 football tournament at Persiaran Ekoflora here yesterday.
The boy, identified as Danish Hizqeel Mohd Hanif, was a player with Shining Academy, a football club based in Pasir Gudang.
It is learnt that the boy collapsed while playing and died without regaining consciousness.
Based on a Shining FC Facebook post, Danish "passed away while playing in a football tournament. He was a player with great potential.
"His loss is deeply felt by the entire Johor football community and his fellow players on the field," the post read.
Shining FC said Danish had represented the club since he was 8-years-old.
The club described him as a passionate and hardworking player who respected his coaches and always brought smiles to his teammates.
"His passing is a great loss to the entire Shining Academy family. His memories, spirit and contributions will always remain in our hearts," it said.
Shining FC is expected to release a statement soon.
Johor Baru (South) police chief Assistant Commissioner Azrul Hisham Mohd Shaffei, who confirmed the incident, said the case was classified as sudden death.
The New Straits Times attempted to contact the boy's parents, who had yet to respond at press time.
Meanwhile, Johor Youth and Sports, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives committee chairman Mohd Hairi Mad Shah reminded sports organisers to prioritise safety measures.
"There is a need for strict safety measures, medical preparedness and health monitoring at all sporting events, especially those involving young athletes.
"Sports competitions in Johor should be organised in consultation with recognised, government-approved sports governing bodies to ensure compliance with regulations, adequate safety measures, effective emergency response plans and the welfare of all participants," he told the NST.
He also extended his condolences to the boy's family.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Private hospital doctor jailed seven years for physical sexual assault on boy
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: A private hospital doctor was sentenced to seven years' jail and seven strokes of the rotan by the Sessions Court today after being found guilty of sexually assaulting an 11-year-old boy four years ago.
Judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli handed down the sentence after ruling that the defence had failed to raise reasonable doubt at the close of its case.
Dr Mohd Syafwan Mohd Soffian, 42, was convicted on seven counts of physical sexual assault under the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
Six of the offences were committed in a clinic room at a private hospital here between Sept 25 and April 10, 2023, while the seventh occurred at the same location at 8pm on Oct 9, 2022.
The offences were framed under Sections 14(a) and 14(d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, which carry a maximum penalty of 20 years' imprisonment and whipping upon conviction.
The court sentenced the accused to five years' jail and one stroke of the rotan for each of the six charges under Section 14(a), together with a two-year police supervision order.
For the remaining charge under Section 14(d), he was sentenced to seven years' jail, one stroke of the rotan and a two-year police supervision order.
The court ordered all prison sentences to run concurrently from today.
The prosecution was led by deputy public prosecutors Ahmad Hafizuddin Manjur Ahmad and Nur Azhani Azman, while the accused was represented by Ahmad Fairuz Jusoh, Ahmad Kamal Abu Bakar and Arik Zakri Abdul Kadir.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three teen girls held over viral 'Superman' motorcycle stunt
Article:
KUANTAN: Three teenage girls who went viral for performing 'Superman' stunts on their motorcycles along Jalan Kuantan Bypass-Gebeng road last week have been arrested.
Video clips showing the
three girls lying flat on their motorcycles
with both legs extended backwards while riding on a wet road surface circulated widely on social media, prompting police action.
Kuantan police chief Assistant Commissioner Ashari Abu Samah said the girls, aged between 18 and 21, were arrested at 8pm yesterday after they turned up at the Kuantan Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department for questioning.
"They allegedly performed the stunt in the early hours of July 16. Acting on information, the police Mobile Patrol Vehicle (MPV) tracked down several individuals believed to have been involved in the activity.
"They were called to the district police headquarters for questioning before the three girls admitted to performing the dangerous stunts," he said, adding that the girls were arrested to assist with investigations.
Ashari said investigations revealed that two of the girls worked as shop assistants, while the third was employed as a hotel receptionist.
Police had seized three Yamaha LC135 motorcycles believed to have been used by the suspects when carrying out the dangerous stunts.
He said they are being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for riding a motorcycle in a dangerous manner that endangered other road users.
The offence carries a maximum penalty of five years' imprisonment and a fine of up to RM15,000 upon conviction.
He warned that police would not compromise with individuals who perform dangerous acts on the road, as such behaviour endangers not only their own safety but also that of other road users.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz dismisses PH Youth resignation call over BN-PN understanding
Article:
KUALA PILAH: Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has dismissed calls by Pakatan Harapan Youth for Barisan Nasional ministers and deputy ministers to resign over the coalition's electoral understanding with Perikatan Nasional in the Negri Sembilan polls.
Responding to the call, Onn Hafiz said PH should apply the same standard in states where BN and PH do not cooperate politically.
"If they want us to resign, then they should also ask us to resign in Johor because we do not have an understanding with PH there.
"If they want BN ministers to resign at the national level, that is a matter for the party's top leadership to decide. But they should also do the same in Perak, Pahang and elsewhere.
"Why be hypocritical and practise double standards?" he told reporters after campaigning for BN candidates in state seats under Kuala Pilah today.
Also present were Juasseh candidate Datuk Ismail Lasim, Seri Menanti candidate Sufian Maradzi, candidate Mohamad Qayyum Abd Jalil, Pilah candidate Lisa Mat Yassin and Johol candidate Datuk Saiful Yazan Sulaiman.
PH Youth had urged BN ministers and deputy ministers to step down from the federal administration following BN's decision to work with PN in the Negri Sembilan polls despite being partners in the unity government at the federal level.
In a joint statement today, the coalition said BN's latest political moves showed that the party was prioritising short-term political interests over its commitment to the unity government led by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
It claimed BN's cooperation with PN had raised doubts over the coalition's loyalty to the federal administration, despite holding key positions including the deputy prime ministership, several ministerial portfolios and other senior government posts.
On a separate matter, Onn Hafiz said feedback from grassroots supporters showed that many Negri Sembilan voters wanted the official minutes of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang special meeting held in April to be made public.
He said the documents should be released immediately so the public could determine the truth surrounding the circumstances leading to the dissolution of the state assembly.
"Based on the feedback I received from the grassroots, many people in Negri Sembilan are demanding that the minutes of the meeting held in April be released as soon as possible so the truth can be seen.
"Don't hide important matters. Let the people of Negri Sembilan see what really happened," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: N9 polls: 'Treachery' claim against me doesn't make sense, says Jalaluddin
Article:
JELEBU: Negri Sembilan Umno liaison committee chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias has questioned accusations by several Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders that he committed an act of treachery.
Jalaluddin, who is the Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate for the Pertang seat, said his actions, along with those of his colleagues, merely involved withdrawing support for Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
He said the accusations were illogical.
"Some PH leaders said I had committed treachery (derhaka). I withdrew my support for the menteri besar, but who exactly was I being treacherous towards? Was I being treacherous towards the menteri besar?
"Having differences with or withdrawing support for the menteri besar cannot be described as disloyalty. Treachery refers to disloyalty towards the ruler or the Undang. I merely withdrew my support for the menteri besar," he said.
He said this after attending the Jelebu Division Community Development Department Skills Day Programme at Felda Pasoh 4 today.
The Jelebu member of Parliament said the matter needed to be understood by the people and urged PH leaders not to twist the issue.
"Do not spin the issue. I have said this many times — do not instil hatred among the people over the weaknesses that we have created.
"Times have changed, and the people are more mature now," he said.
Touching on an incident during nomination yesterday, when he was booed and jeered by PH supporters, Jalaluddin also questioned their behaviour.
"Yesterday, during the announcement in Jelebu, when the PH candidate was announced, I applauded. Why did I applaud? Was I supporting the PH candidate? No. I was respecting and recognising the democratic process and the right of any party to contest.
"But when my name was announced as the Pertang candidate, they booed and jeered at me. Is this the attitude and mentality of the people today?
"So they should reflect on that themselves. But it is alright, in a way, it is good. The people can decide who is worthy of their votes and who is not," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar pledges RM15.3 million boost for Perak religious and pondok schools
Article:
IPOH: The Madani government will continue to enhance and uplift religious and pondok schools nationwide, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said pondok schools once played a vital role in providing education, but fell behind when rapid changes took place and they were unable to acquire knowledge in other fields.
In this regard, Anwar said he was proud of the religious teachers, pondok school leaders and tok guru for preserving Islamic traditions and classical religious texts while being prepared to explore new disciplines such as computer technology and artificial intelligence (AI).
He thanked the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC) and the Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU) under the Prime Minister's Department for their cooperation in helping to improve these institutions.
"I give my assurance, insya-Allah, as we continue to remain healthy and the economy remains like this, as I told Sheikh Wazir Che Awang Al-Makki, insya-Allah, we will enhance and uplift religious schools and pondok schools throughout our country," he said.
Also present were Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan, and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.
The prime minister said the Madani government will continue the digital transformation initiative for Islamic educational institutions that was launched in March.
He noted that several pondok schools and maahad, or religious secondary schools, had been equipped with new technologies to ensure students are prepared to acquire new knowledge.
"So, I appreciate the cooperation extended by these schools, and today we will continue to increase the allocations and assistance as announced," he said.
Anwar advised students at the schools to study diligently and prove that they can compete not only in terms of intellect, competence and proficiency in specific disciplines such as syariah and Arabic, but also in emerging fields of knowledge.
At the event, the prime minister announced that RM15 million had been approved for pondok and religious schools throughout Perak for 2026, while RM300,000 was approved specifically for Sekolah Agama Bantuan Kerajaan Maahad Al-Ummah in Chemor.
"We approved allocations for 115 pondok schools in Perak last year. This year, we are increasing the allocation by providing RM15 million for all pondok schools in Perak that did not receive sufficient assistance previously," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia sends 'very strong' diplomatic note over territorial claim
Article:
SEREMBAN: Malaysia has sent a "very strong" diplomatic note to a country that claimed part of its territory belongs to it, Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said today.
Without naming the country, Mohamad said the claim was baseless as the people of the territory had held a referendum recognised by the United Nations (UN), with the outcome subsequently affirmed through a United Nations Security Council resolution.
"As foreign minister, there are times when I have to engage in disputes. Recently, I sent a very strong diplomatic note to the country concerned because it claimed that an area within our country belongs to them.
"They seem to have forgotten that the people of that territory held a referendum, which was recognised by the UN, and a United Nations Security Council resolution affirmed that the people chose to be with us.
"So there should no longer be any question of saying that the territory belongs to them," he said.
He was speaking at the armed forces well-being programme held in conjunction with the Sendayan Air Base Family Day Carnival here today.
Mohamad also called on members of the armed forces to continue defending the country's territory and sovereign rights against any threats.
"Defend our territory and our sovereign rights to the very last drop of blood because that is the dignity of our nation and country, God willing," he said.
Mohamad said the government was strengthening the country's defence capabilities, particularly in the South China Sea, through the addition of surveillance assets.
"That is why I requested the most advanced radar system to be placed on Layang-Layang Island because without clear visibility of the area, it would be difficult for us to operate.
"Thankfully, our unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) have arrived. They are also very important assets for monitoring our area," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman fined RM5,000 for allowing undocumented migrant to stay at shop
Article:
PASIR MAS: A woman was fined RM5,000 by the Sessions Court here today for allowing an undocumented migrant to remain at her sundry shop on July 9.
The 30-year-old pleaded guilty to the charge before Sessions Court Judge Zulkifli Abdullah.
According to the charge, Nor Hapiza Zakaria allowed a 23-year-old Pakistani man, identified as Asif Iqbal, to remain at Cikk Za Mini Mart, PT 9767, Kampung Pusu 40, Cherang Hangus, here at 12.30pm on July 9.
She was charged under Section 55E(1) of the Immigration Act 1959/63, punishable under Section 55E(2) of the same Act.
In mitigation, Nor Hapiza, who was unrepresented, pleaded for a lighter sentence.
Prosecuting, Siti Khaidijah Khairuddin urged the court to impose an appropriate sentence as a lesson and deterrent.
In delivering the sentence, Zulkifli reminded the accused that she should be aware of and comply with the country's laws.
"There is nothing wrong with employing foreign workers, but it must be done in accordance with the law," he said.
The court fined Nor Hapiza RM5,000, and she paid the fine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM to explain KWAP's eFishery investment in Dewan Negara tomorrow
Article:
IPOH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will address questions surrounding Retirement Fund Incorporated (KWAP) and its investment in eFishery when he appears in the Dewan Negara tomorrow.
Anwar said he would provide a detailed explanation based on facts rather than making premature comments on the matter.
"I will issue a statement and answer that question in Parliament, in the Dewan Negara, tomorrow.
"KWAP indeed comes under the overall purview of the government, but it is a financial institution managing pension funds. It has its own investment panel and board of directors.
"They do not report directly to us. However, I do not want to make excuses. Let us present the facts tomorrow in the Dewan Negara," he said.
He was speaking to reporters after attending the Madani Kita Programme at Dewan Komuniti Taman Perpaduan here today.
KWAP, in a statement yesterday, said its investment in Indonesian aquaculture technology company eFishery amounted to RM163.4 million, representing about 2.51 per cent of the company's total shareholding.
The fund said it was a minority shareholder, while the majority of eFishery's shares were held by other investors, including major global institutional investors who were also affected by the alleged misconduct.
KWAP
said it had conducted an internal investigation following the discovery of irregularities and reviewed its investment processes, post-investment monitoring arrangements, and available information throughout the investment period.
"In light of the matters raised, we wish to provide further clarity on the actions undertaken following our internal review, as well as the measures being implemented to safeguard the interests of the fund," it said.
The fund added that appropriate follow-up actions had been taken in accordance with its internal governance and accountability framework.
KWAP said the wider eFishery case involved alleged deliberate financial manipulation and misrepresentation in the company's financial reporting.
The
Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission
(MACC) has launched an investigation into the reported investment losses suffered by the KWAP.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jalan Tun Razak tree fall victim to be laid to rest in Batu Pahat
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The remains of Mohd Amir Shazwan Saion, 43, who died after a tree fell on his vehicle along Jalan Tun Razak last night, will be taken back to his hometown in Batu Pahat, Johor, for burial.
His younger brother, Mohd Amir Adham Saion, 38, said the family made the decision following the completion of the post-mortem examination at Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL).
He said the arrangement would allow their elderly parents, who remained in their hometown, to see their son for the final time.
"Our mother and father are still in the village. I told them we would bring the remains home so they would not need to come to HKL.
"This will allow them to see their beloved son for the last time," he said when met at HKL.
Amir Adham said his brother was a well-liked person who was always willing to help others, including family members and friends.
"He was our eldest brother. I am the third child, and he was the one who helped the family the most. Whenever we had any problems, he would always be there for us, even though we are all grown up.
"My brother was easy to get along with and always willing to help people. His friends in the village are still close to him until today, including his colleagues at work," he said.
He said he never expected the last time the four siblings gathered at their hometown during the Johor election last week would become their final memory of their brother.
Amir Adham said he rushed to HKL after receiving a call from his sister-in-law, the victim's wife, informing him that his brother was in critical condition.
"I was at home in Selayang, Selangor, when I received the call from my sister-in-law telling me that my brother was critically injured at HKL.
"At that time, I did not know what had happened. I only found out the incident had gone viral this morning after reading the news and being informed by friends who had seen the reports," he said.
He said he arrived at HKL at 1am today, but his brother died around 4am after several hours of treatment.
Amir Adham said his brother was believed to have been on his way to return a company vehicle to the office when the incident occurred.
He added that many of his brother's friends, as well as the owner of the limousine company where he worked, had visited the hospital and pledged their support to the family.
"His boss also came and promised to provide whatever assistance was needed. I hope everything goes smoothly," he said.
Meanwhile, checks at the scene found that the fallen tree debris had been cleared and traffic was moving as usual.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 14 families keep Pulau Raya's 200-year heritage alive
Article:
TANAH MERAH: Pulau Raya, one of Kelantan's smallest villages, is home to 14 Chinese families preserving a way of life that has endured for more than two centuries.
Village head Wee Thin Kim, 51, said the island, believed to be more than 200 years old, had changed significantly over the decades.
He said Pulau Raya was once home to both Malay and Chinese families, but today only the Chinese community remains.
"I became the village head in 2018. Although we are a small community, we are determined to preserve the island's history and traditions for future generations," he said.
Wee said the village has only 14 houses, including six traditional homes, many of which are built using bamboo and timber.
[[nid:1492363]]
"Some residents continue to maintain these traditional houses because they are part of our heritage.
"We hope the younger generation will appreciate and preserve them," he said.
Despite its long history, Pulau Raya has no public facilities such as a school or bank.
"Most residents earn a living as fruit farmers, cultivating durian orchards, rubber smallholdings and other crops.
"Their produce is transported to nearby Tanah Merah, about a 10-minute drive away via the bridge connecting the island to Kampung Belimbing," he said.
For businessman Wee Iu Kee, 41, Pulau Raya is more than just a place to live.
The father of four said his family had lived on the island for generations and still occupied the ancestral home built by his forefathers.
"This is my family's inherited home. My great-grandfather settled here many years ago, and we have remained on the island ever since," he said.
He said his late father and ancestors devoted their lives to farming, and the family continues to care for about 50 durian trees planted by previous generations.
"My children are growing up surrounded by the same environment that shaped our family.
"I hope they will continue to value our roots and keep Pulau Raya's history alive," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Samsung cuts US jobs, offers relocations ahead of HQ move
Article:
SEOUL: Samsung Electronics has cut jobs at its US display, phone and other consumer electronics operations — affecting workers mainly in New Jersey and Texas, according to documents and two people familiar with the matter.
The South Korean tech giant said on Sunday in a statement to Reuters that 739 roles in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey, have been affected by plans by Samsung Electronics America (SEA) — which is focused on consumer electronics and does not include chips — to move its headquarters to Texas.
A majority of people affected have received relocation offers, but others were let go, it added without elaborating.
At SEA's Plano, Texas office, some 100 workers including staff in its mobile division, have been let go, according to one person who said they were among the employees laid off. Sources declined to be identified because of the sensitivity of the issue.
The cuts — though related to the shift in headquarters — underscore diverging fortunes within the South Korean tech giant with its chip division skyrocketing to record profit but its consumer electronics units languishing as chip costs surge.
Samsung's decision to shift SEA's headquarters is striking because SEA employees in New Jersey only moved to new offices with much fanfare less than a year ago. SEA employs about 1,200 workers in New Jersey, according to a press release by US Representative Josh Gottheimer, who attended an event to mark the opening of the new offices in September.
While the precise extent of the layoffs at SEA could not be learned, documents seen by Reuters show that the unit notified some employees on June 30 of an "enterprise-wide reduction-in-force", adding that there were a "significant number of impacts."
LinkedIn posts reviewed by Reuters also show more than 30 workers, including senior sales and marketing officials in both Texas and New Jersey as well as a few in other US locations, said they have been let go or left the company over the past couple of weeks.
Details about the job losses at SEA have not previously been reported.
Samsung said in its statement that the shift of SEA's headquarters "may lead to changes in our workforce structure, such as employees who are unable to relocate, or certain functions that are optimized to ensure our roles align to key business priorities."
CHIP DIVISION SOARS, BUT MOBILE MAY POST LOSS
Samsung has flagged it will likely post a 19-fold jump in second-quarter profit on strong AI-driven chip demand. It also announced plans last month to invest hundreds of billions of dollars in new chip plants.
Its mobile division, however, is expected to post its first-ever loss, as it grapples with intense competition from Apple. Chinese rivals like TCL and Hisense are also challenging Samsung in televisions and home appliances. Higher chip costs due to the AI boom have weighed on earnings for all of its consumer electronics products.
Samsung's job cuts mirror moves by other global firms including Microsoft, Amazon and Meta, which have shed jobs while redirecting spending toward AI infrastructure.
It has also joined Tesla, Oracle and other tech companies in moving headquarters or major operations to Texas, known for lower taxes and business-friendly regulations. The state is already home to Samsung's chip factories and a mobile hub in Plano.
Samsung workers are concerned the recent job cuts could be followed by additional layoffs and a consolidation of the company's appliance, home entertainment and mobile divisions, as it focuses resources on chips, a current SEA employee said.
Samsung said in its statement there was currently no broad, global restructuring underway within its consumer product business.
The relocation of the unit's headquarters is intended to foster "stronger collaboration and optimize the organization by bringing more teams together within a growing technology and AI ecosystem," it said.
Samsung Electronics had 11,770 employees in the United States as of the end of 2025, which includes workers in its chip division.
Samsung's IT services affiliate Samsung SDS America , has flagged that 179 roles could be cut at Ridgefield Park, New Jersey, according to a June notice required under the state's laws.
Those personnel changes are due to the relocation of Samsung SDS' North American headquarters and had nothing to do with layoffs or restructuring, Samsung said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Syed Saddiq to focus on marriage before deciding on Muda leadership return [WATCH]
Article:
MUAR: Muar member of parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman has yet to consider reclaiming the leadership of Muda following his recent court acquittal.
Instead, the former youth and sports minister said he would focus on the next chapter of his life, which is building a family.
He said the decision was made after spending more than a decade focusing entirely on politics since 2014.
Syed Saddiq is engaged to actress and singer
Bella Astillah
.
"Honestly, right now I want to focus on building a family first. This is not a safe answer; it is an honest and sincere answer, straight from the heart," he said after presenting aid at the Muar parliamentary service centre here today.
"Since I started in politics in 2014 or 2015, I have not stopped. During that period, it was not just me going through it; my whole family went through the same thing," he said.
In November 2023, Syed Saddiq announced he was
stepping down as Muda president
with immediate effect to clear his name following a court conviction, adding that he would not be involved in party politics while his court case was ongoing.
However, he assured that he would continue to support Muda as an ordinary member and work closely with its members.
Meanwhile, he clarified that he would never neglect his duties as an MP to fulfil the mandate given by the people. He said his responsibilities in Parliament and as an ordinary Muda member would continue until Parliament is dissolved.
"Muda today is led by Amira Aisya (Abd Aziz). The party doesn't belong to me alone because there is an election process that needs to be followed," he said.
Syed Saddiq is the founder and former president of Muda, a youth-focused political party he established in 2020.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pesona Metro bags RM247.5mil hospital job
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pesona Metro Holdings Bhd has secured a RM247.5 million contract to build a private hospital in Setia Alam, Selangor.
Pesona Metro said its wholly-owned Pesona Metro Sdn Bhd had accepted a Letter of Award from Setia Alam Medical Centre Sdn Bhd (SAMC) for the execution and completion of piling, basement, main building and external works for the project.
"The project comprises a basement level accommodating car park bays together with mechanical and electrical space and supporting facilities, along with three blocks of varying heights," it said.
SAMC is a subsidiary of Mega First Corp Bhd and is principally involved in the development and operation of a medical centre.
Pesona Metro managing director Wie Hock Beng said the contract marked another milestone for the company as it continued to expand its footprint in healthcare-related construction.
Wie said with the latest win, Pesona Metro's outstanding order book increased to RM2.4 billion across nine ongoing projects, providing the company with continued earnings visibility over the medium term.
"Moving forward, we remain focused on replenishing and growing our construction order book, balancing risk and reward in assessing new opportunities," he said.
The proposed development comprises three hospital blocks with supporting facilities and multi-storey car parks, located in Setia Alam, Shah Alam, Selangor.
Construction is scheduled to commence on July 28 this year and is expected to be completed within 30 months by Jan 28, 2029.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jalaluddin thanks Perikatan for backing BN to lead Negeri Sembilan if alliance wins
Article:
JELEBU, July 19 — Negeri Sembilan Umno chief Datuk Jalaluddin Alias today thanked Perikatan Nasional (PN) for what he said was the coalition’s early decision to leave the state’s menteri besar post to Barisan Nasional (BN) should the two succeed in forming the next state government after the August 1 election.
He said BN and PN had yet to discuss who should become menteri besar or how executive council positions would be allocated, with both coalitions choosing to focus on winning the election first.
“We never discussed who should become menteri besar. In fact, I would like to thank the PN leadership for declaring early that they would leave the menteri besar’s post to Umno and BN.
“We (BN and PN) have not discussed who the menteri besar candidate will be. Neither have we discussed the allocation of exco seats because our priority is to achieve victory first,” he was quoted by news outlet
Free Malaysia Today
as telling reporters after attending the Kemas Skills Day programme in Felda Pasoh 4 here.
Jalaluddin, who is also BN’s candidate for the Pertang state seat, said discussions on appointments could only take place after the coalition had secured enough seats to form the state government.
“Our goal is to win as many seats as possible first. Only then will we discuss positions that can strengthen the state government and not to satisfy any personal ambitions,” he was quoted as saying.
“We want to form an administration capable of governing the state well. If someone is not qualified for a position, we will not appoint them simply to fulfil a quota.”
Asked whether he was among those being considered for the menteri besar’s post, Jalaluddin said BN had several qualified leaders capable of taking on the role.
He named Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan and Seri Menanti candidate Sufian Maradzi among those who could be considered.
“It is up to the BN chairman to decide. Don’t worry, we have many qualified leaders,” he said.
Negeri Sembilan voters will head to the polls on August 1 to elect representatives for all 36 state seats.
BN and PN are contesting the election under an electoral understanding, with BN fielding candidates in 25 seats and PN in the remaining 11, with no overlap between the two coalitions.

Title: Pakistani man in Kelantan loses Proton Gen-2, fined RM20,000 after giving four undocumented migrants a ride
Article:
KOTA BHARU, July 19 — A Proton Gen-2 ride cost a Pakistani man more than just a set of wheels after the Sessions Court in Pasir Mas, Kelantan today ordered the car forfeited to the government and fined him RM20,000 for allowing four undocumented migrants to remain inside it.
Judge Zulkifli Abdullah imposed the fine on Muhammad Zaman, 30, and ordered him to serve eight months in prison if he fails to pay,
Harian Metro
reported.
Muhammad pled guilty to allowing the four undocumented migrants to be in the car at a parking area along Jalan Parit Dalam in Kota Bharu at about 7.10am on July 12.
The four comprised an Indian national and three Pakistani men aged between 23 and 37.
He was charged under Section 55E(1) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for permitting undocumented migrants to enter or remain in premises or a place under his control.
The offence carries a penalty under Section 55E(2) of a fine of between RM5,000 and RM30,000, imprisonment of up to 12 months, or both, for each undocumented migrant involved.
Muhammad was not represented by a lawyer.
The prosecution was conducted by prosecuting officer Siti Khaidijah Khairuddin.
The court also ordered that the Proton Gen-2 used in the offence be forfeited to the government.

Title: ‘No excuses’: Anwar to explain KWAP’s RM200m eFishery investment loss in Dewan Negara tomorrow
Article:
IPOH, July 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he will explain in the Dewan Negara tomorrow the reported RM200 million loss involving Retirement Fund Incorporated’s (KWAP) investment in Indonesian company eFishery.
According to
Sinar Harian
reported that Anwar, who is also finance minister, said he would set out the facts surrounding the investment and would not offer excuses over the matter.
“I will answer the question in the Dewan Negara tomorrow,” was quoted as saying Madani programme the Taman Perpaduan Community Hall here today.
Anwar said KWAP had its own investment panel and board and did not report directly to the government, but insisted he would not use that as an excuse.
Aquaculture technology eFishery developed smart automatic feeding systems that used sensors to distribute feed and monitor consumption at fish and shrimp farms.
The Finance Ministry previously told Parliament that KWAP was believed to have fallen victim to an organised fraud involving the manipulation of eFishery’s financial reports by the company’s management.
The ministry said the investor consortium, including KWAP, had initiated legal action and fund-recovery efforts, while reviewing internal governance and strengthening investment controls.
The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission said yesterday that it had formed an investigation team to conduct a comprehensive review of the investment loss.

Title: Zizie Izette to learn verdict tomorrow in RM2.8m corruption abetment case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad will learn her fate tomorrow when the Sessions Court delivers its verdict on three charges of abetting in a corruption case involving RM2.8 million linked to a RM150 million investment in Public Mutual Berhad unit trusts.
Sessions Court Judge Rosli Ahmad is scheduled to deliver the decision at 9am at the conclusion of the defence case.
Zizie Izette, 47, the wife of the late Kinabatangan Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin, is facing three charges of abetting her husband in obtaining cash bribes amounting to RM2.8 million.
The defence closed its case after Zizie Izette completed her testimony on March 5. Bung Moktar had testified on September 2, 2025, after the couple failed to overturn the Court of Appeal’s decision ordering them to enter their defence.
On November 18, 2024, the Court of Appeal allowed the prosecution’s appeal against the September 7, 2023, High Court’s decision, acquitting the couple.
The High Court had overturned the Sessions Court’s September 2, 2022 ruling, which had found that the prosecution had established a prima facie case and ordered Bung Moktar and Zizie Izette to enter their defence.
However, on January 16, the Sessions Court acquitted Bung Moktar of all three charges after the prosecution decided not to proceed with the case following the former politician’s death on December 5, 2025.
Under the first charge, Zizie Izette is accused of abetting Bung Moktar, who was then the non-executive chairman of Felcra Berhad, in obtaining a RM2.2 million cash bribe from Public Mutual Berhad investment agent Madhi Abdul Hamid.
The alleged bribe was an inducement for Bung Moktar to secure the approval of the then Finance Minister II for Felcra Berhad to invest RM150 million in Public Mutual Berhad unit trust products.
Under the second charge, she is accused of abetting Bung Moktar in obtaining RM262,500 from Madhi through a Public Islamic Treasures Growth Fund (PITGF) account registered in her name for the same purpose.
She also faces a third charge of abetting her husband in obtaining RM337,500 from another Public Mutual Berhad investment agent, Norhaili Ahmad Mokhtar, through a Public Ittikal Sequel Fund (PITSEQ) account registered in her name, also in connection with the same investment.
The offences were allegedly committed at the Public Bank Berhad Taman Melawati branch on June 12 and June 19, 2015.
The charges are framed under Section 28(1)(c) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009, which provides for a maximum prison sentence of 20 years and a fine of five times the value of the gratification or RM10,000, whichever is higher, upon conviction.
The prosecution is conducted ed by Deputy Public Prosecutors Law Chin How and Mohammad Fadhly Mohd Zamry, while lawyer Datuk Seri K. Kumaraendran represented Zizie Izette. — Bernama

Title: From AI prompting to vehicle maintenance, thousands of free courses offered to Malaysians during National Training Week
Article:
SANDAKAN, July 19 — Thousands of free courses and skills development activities will be offered to all Malaysians, regardless of age, in conjunction with the fourth edition of the National Training Week (NTW) 2026, which begins tomorrow.
The Human Resource Development Corporation (HRD Corp), in a statement today, said the programme, organised in collaboration with the Ministry of Human Resources (Kesuma), aims to strengthen lifelong learning, and information can be accessed at
www.nationaltrainingweek.gov.my
.
The launch of the programme, themed ‘Pelajari, Alami, Kuasai’ (Learn, Experience, Master), was officiated by Deputy Minister of Human Resources Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan at the Sandakan Community Hall here last night.
“The NTW is not merey an annual programme, but serves as a national movement to instil a culture of lifelong learning among Malaysians,” Khairul Firdaus said when launching the programme.
He added that the selection of Sandakan as the launch venue recognises Sabah’s crucial role in the nation’s human capital agenda, with training opportunities made widely available to employees, entrepreneurs, gig workers, homemakers and retirees.
Among the contemporary courses offered are programmes on artificial intelligence (AI) prompting, digital marketing, AI animation, drone operations, autism support, language courses and vehicle maintenance.
HRD Corp chairman Datuk Rusli Jaafar said in the same statement that close collaboration with industry players and training institutions had enabled the free learning opportunities to be offered in an inclusive manner.
The fourth edition of this campaign also featured the presentation of special incentives to 149 top-performing 2025 SPM students from the Batu Sapi parliamentary constituency to encourage them to further improve their academic and career skills.
Visitors to the carnival also stand the chance to take part in various interactive activities, including knowledge-sharing sessions, career development advisory services and exhibitions by various training agencies.
The NTW 2026 roadshow will continue across the country before culminating in Penang on July 25 and 26.
A check of the programme’s portal found that the public can participate in the courses either online or at selected institutions, depending on the nature of the course. — Bernama

Title: ‘Win, but not by breaking the law’: Loke urges road safety during Negeri Sembilan campaign
Article:
SEREMBAN, July 19 — Road safety and traffic compliance must be strictly observed throughout the ongoing 16th Negeri Sembilan state election campaign, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke.
He stressed that these rules are non-negotiable for preventing accidents and ensuring an orderly, secure environment.
The DAP secretary-general said he has personally instructed his party’s campaign teams to wear helmets at all times when riding motorcycles during the campaign period.
“We want to ensure this campaign stays accident-free. Every party wants to win, but not at the cost of flouting traffic laws. The bare minimum is wearing a helmet.
“During yesterday’s nomination day, I joined a convoy from Pekan Titi to Kuala Klawang. I made it clear upfront — no helmet, no convoy. This is a basic requirement that everyone must adhere to,” he said here today.
He was speaking to reporters after a walkabout with Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidates, Ho Weng Wah (Temiang), Lee Kai Yet (Mambau), Siau Meow Kong (Rahang), Nicole Tan (Bukit Kepayang), S. Mugunthan (Seremban Jaya), and Chew Seh Yong (Lobak), at Pasar Besar Seremban today.
In a separate reminder, Loke, who is also the Seremban Member of Parliament, warned all candidates and supporters against riding on the tailgates of moving four-wheel-drive vehicles, calling the practice reckless.
He further called on all parties and leaders on the campaign trail to lead by example and strictly observe traffic regulations throughout the campaign.
The Negeri Sembilan state assembly was dissolved on June 5, with the Election Commission setting August 1 as polling day and July 28 for early voting.
A total of 889,490 registered voters are eligible to vote in this state election, comprising 867,151 ordinary voters, 16,884 military personnel and their spouses, and 5,455 police officers as early voters.
For the latest news on the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, please visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9. — Bernama

Title: Putrajaya to Muar: Syed Saddiq says RM650,000 raised after completing 170km charity run
Article:
